attacking_style: Launches attacks in final 10km, typically during chase hesitation or on penultimate climbs (8.5km at Liège, 4km to go at Suisse). Prefers reduced group sprints over solo efforts.
team_tactics: BORA operates coordinated GC plays with Vlasov as protected rider or designated finisher; teammates (Lechner, Fulsang) split groups on penultimate climbs to set up reduced sprints for him.
terrain_preference: Thrives on stage races with multiple climbing tests where attrition creates reduced sprints. Penultimate climbs that fragment groups suit him better than summit finishes requiring sustained acceleration.
vulnerability: Cannot bridge gaps to truly elite climbers when they attack decisively (Evenepoel gap at Liège). Lacks explosive acceleration to distance rivals on final climbs, relying instead on teammate-setup and positioning.
key_patterns: Wins from reduced sprints after team splits peloton, not solo attacks (Suisse, Romandie)
Hunts bonus seconds and positions for podium rather than stage wins at Grand Tours
Attacks during chase hesitation windows in 4-10km range on decisive climbs
Benefits from deliberate BORA 1-2 tactical coordination with co-leaders or strong domestiques
form_trajectory: positive: Progressive results from Romandie (2nd) to Suisse (win) in 2022, then Vuelta podium aspirations in 2023 suggest sustained Grand Tour GC capabilities
built_from: 2023 La Vuelta Ciclista a Espana Stage 9
